_comment: Enforcement + run-span hooks. Scope: capture the sensor-ran flag and emit ONE run-level OTLp span at stop. It does NOT reconstruct per-tool spans — kiro-cli has no native OTLP (verified), so the rich tool-level trace comes from a post-run converter that reads the session .jsonl and builds the span tree. Hooks stay minimal; the JSONL converter does trace reconstruction. Requires otel-cli on PATH and OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT (e.g. http://tempo.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:4317). See measuring-harness-quality.md 'Adapter design'.
_attributes_note: Use OTel GenAI semconv for the OBTAINABLE model/tool layer (gen_ai.operation.name, gen_ai.agent.name, gen_ai.tool.name) + a harness.* namespace for CI-only signals. RUN_ID correlates the span set to the eval trace file. BLOCKED today (kiro issue #6319, no native OTLP): token usage, per-call latency, error rate are runtime-internal and not exposed to hooks or the session JSONL — do NOT emit gen_ai.usage.* or latency.

agentSpawn._why: Open the run span. gen_ai.operation.name=invoke_agent, gen_ai.agent.name, gen_ai.request.model.

postToolUse._why: Record tool_result + sensor-ran flag for harness.sensors_ran.
postToolUse.command: bash -c 'RID=$(cat "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/trace-run-id.txt" 2>/dev/null); CMD=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.command 2>/dev/null); echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "cargo (clippy|test)|npm (run build|test)" && touch "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/trace-sensors.txt"; printf "{\"ev\":\"tool_result\",\"run\":\"%s\",\"ts\":%s}\n" "$RID" "$(date +%s%N)" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/trace-events.jsonl"'

stop._why: Close the run span and emit OTLP to Tempo. harness.verdict / iterations / sensors_ran / verification_gap. Replace verdict/iterations parsing with your output-contract reader.
stop.command: bash -c 'RID=$(cat "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/trace-run-id.txt" 2>/dev/null); SENSORS=$([ -f "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/trace-sensors.txt" ] && echo true || echo false); if command -v otel-cli >/dev/null 2>&1; then otel-cli span --service harness --name "agent.run" --attrs "gen_ai.operation.name=invoke_agent,gen_ai.agent.name=${AGENT_NAME:-unknown},harness.sensors_ran=${SENSORS},harness.run_id=${RID}" 2>/dev/null || true; fi; rm -f "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/trace-sensors.txt" 2>/dev/null'
